jobs.title: Scaffold standalone Leptos+WASM project with Trunk
jobs.body: Create a new Rust project (cargo init) — this is a standalone repo, not part of the chuggernaut workspace. Set up Cargo.toml with [lib] crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"], dependencies: leptos, wasm-bindgen, web-sys, js-sys, gloo-net, gloo-timers, serde, serde_json, chrono (with wasm feature). Add a Trunk.toml with a dev-server proxy: [[proxy]] rewrite = "/api/" backend = "{{DISPATCHER_URL}}". Include a minimal main() that mounts an <App/> component into the document body. Verify `trunk build` produces working .wasm + JS glue in dist/.

jobs.title: Fetch API manifest and generate typed client at build time
jobs.body:
Add a build.rs that fetches the API manifest from {{DISPATCHER_URL}}/api.json (the dispatcher URL is provided via CHUGGERNAUT_API_URL env var, defaulting to http://localhost:8080). Save the raw manifest to OUT_DIR/api.json. Also check in a snapshot at api-snapshot.json so builds work without a running dispatcher — build.rs should prefer the live fetch but fall back to the snapshot.

The manifest contains 'routes' (array of route specs with method, path, summary, request/response type names, query params, inline request schemas, and SSE event definitions), 'schema' (full JSON Schema with $defs for all domain types), and 'error_response' (the error envelope type name).

From the schema $defs, generate Rust structs and enums in OUT_DIR/api_types.rs with #[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Deserialize, serde::Serialize)]. Walk the schema to handle: string enums, tagged enums (check 'tag' key in the schema), nested structs, Option fields (from non-required properties), Vec fields (from array types), and DateTime<Utc> (from string fields with format: date-time, map to chrono). Include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/api_types.rs")) from src/api/types.rs.

From the routes array, generate an ApiClient struct in OUT_DIR/api_client.rs with one method per route. Derive method signatures from the route specs: use 'params' for path parameters, 'query' for optional query parameters, 'request' or 'request_inline' for the request body, and 'response' for the return type. Each method uses gloo-net to make the HTTP call and deserialize the response. The client constructor takes a base_url: String.

Add a unit test that the generated types round-trip deserialize from example JSON.

jobs.title: Implement SSE client with typed event deserialization
jobs.body:
Create src/sse.rs. Use web-sys EventSource to connect to {base_url}/events. The SSE event types and their payload schemas are defined in the API manifest under the /events route's 'sse_events' field — generate an SseEvent enum variant for each, deserializing payloads using the generated types from task 2. Do not hardcode event names or shapes; derive them from the manifest.

Expose a connect(base_url) -> (ReadSignal<SseEvent>, ReadSignal<ConnectionStatus>) API where ConnectionStatus is Connected | Reconnecting(u32) | Disconnected. Handle reconnection with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, cap 30s). On reconnect the server resends the full state so no special resync logic is needed.

jobs.title: Build reactive state store from SSE events
jobs.body:
Create src/store.rs. On mount, call sse::connect() to open the SSE stream. The SSE snapshot event delivers the full server state — use its payload shape (defined in the API manifest's sse_events) to determine which collections to maintain as signals. For each top-level collection in the snapshot payload, create an RwSignal holding the appropriate map or list of generated types.

Process incremental SSE events by upserting or removing entries in the corresponding signal. The event names and payload shapes come from the manifest — the store should handle each event type that sse_events defines.

Provide derived signals that group and count jobs by their state enum (the state enum's variants are defined in the schema). Expose the ApiClient from task 2 via the store context so components can call mutations and the SSE stream handles the reactive update.

Wrap in a StoreProvider context component so children use use_context::<Store>().

jobs.title: Implement client-side routing and top-level layout
jobs.body: Set up leptos_router in src/app.rs. Routes: / -> JobBoard, /dag -> DagView, /timeline -> Timeline, /jobs/{key} -> JobDetail. Add a persistent sidebar nav with links to each route and a Chuggernaut logo/wordmark at the top. The sidebar should highlight the active route. Below the nav links, show summary stats from store derived signals (counts per state). Use a <main> content area that fills remaining viewport. Add dark/light theme toggle that persists to localStorage. Include an SSE connection status indicator.

jobs.title: Create job board component with state-grouped columns
jobs.body: Build src/components/job_board.rs — the main dashboard view. Render a kanban-style board with one column per job state variant (the variants come from the generated state enum in the schema — do not hardcode column names). Each column header shows the state name and count. Jobs render as cards showing key fields from the generated Job type: use the schema to determine which fields are most useful for a summary card (title, key, state, priority at minimum). Cards are sorted by priority descending within each column. Use Leptos derived signals so columns re-render only when their subset of jobs changes. Clicking a card navigates to /jobs/{key} detail view.

jobs.title: Create DAG visualization component using SVG
jobs.body: Build src/components/dag_view.rs. Render the job dependency graph as an SVG. Use a layered layout algorithm (topological sort, assign layers by longest-path, minimize crossings with barycenter heuristic). Draw job nodes as rounded rects colored by state. Draw dependency edges as SVG paths with arrowheads. Assign a distinct color to each job state variant from the generated enum. Nodes are clickable and navigate to the job detail view. The graph should auto-resize to fit the container. Provide pan (mouse drag) and zoom (scroll wheel) via SVG viewBox manipulation. Re-layout reactively when the deps signal changes.

jobs.title: Create activity log and journal timeline component
jobs.body: Build src/components/timeline.rs. Render a unified, reverse-chronological timeline. Use the generated ApiClient to fetch journal entries (the route and response type are in the manifest). For per-job activity, the job detail response includes activity entries — consult the schema for the activity entry shape. Merge and sort by timestamp. Each entry shows relative timestamp, kind badge, message text, and job key as a link. Auto-scroll to newest on update. Support filtering by job key and entry kind. Limit rendered entries to 200 with 'load more'. Used both standalone at /timeline and embedded in the job detail view.

jobs.title: Create job detail view with actions panel
jobs.body:
Build src/components/job_detail.rs. Route: /jobs/{key}. Fetch full detail via the generated ApiClient's get-job method on mount, then stay updated via store signals.

Top section: render the key fields from the Job type (consult the schema). If a claim exists for this job in the store's claims map, show the claim details (worker, lease deadline countdown, etc. — fields are in the ClaimState schema). Middle section: tabbed view with Activity (embedded timeline filtered to this job), Channel (live channel status from store + input box using the channel send route from the manifest), and Deps (mini DAG from the deps route showing immediate neighbors). Bottom section: action buttons derived from the available mutation routes in the manifest (requeue, close/revoke). Show confirmation dialog before destructive actions.

jobs.title: Add CSS styling with Tailwind-compatible utility classes
jobs.body: Create static assets in style/. Use a build-time CSS approach compatible with Trunk: either a hand-written CSS file with utility classes matching the component markup, or integrate tailwindcss via a Trunk pre-build hook. Define a color palette: primary (indigo), success (emerald), warning (amber), danger (rose), neutral (slate). Map each job state variant to a color — derive the set of states from the generated enum rather than hardcoding. Style all components for both light and dark themes. Ensure the kanban board is responsive — stack columns vertically on narrow viewports. Style the DAG SVG nodes and edges. Add transitions for state changes. Ensure text is readable and accessible (WCAG AA contrast ratios).

jobs.title: Integration test: seed fixture, verify UI renders correct state
jobs.body: Create tests/integration.rs using wasm-bindgen-test. The test needs a running dispatcher — configure via CHUGGERNAUT_API_URL env var (same as the build.rs). Seed the dispatcher with test jobs using the create-job route from the manifest. Mount the App component in a headless browser context. Assert: the job board shows the correct number of cards per column, the DAG view renders the expected number of nodes and edges, the timeline shows journal entries, and clicking a job card navigates to its detail view showing the correct title and state. Test SSE reactivity: use the requeue route to move a job, assert the UI updates within 2 seconds.
